title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
subline: System 1 runs fast. System 2 runs slow. Both lie.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- Two systems. Top half is System 1 (fast, light, fluid curves). Bottom half is System 2 (slow, dark, geometric). -->
  <defs>
    <clipPath id="topHalf">
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="1600" height="1170"/>
    </clipPath>
    <clipPath id="botHalf">
      <rect x="0" y="1230" width="1600" height="1170"/>
    </clipPath>
  </defs>
  <g clip-path="url(#topHalf)">
    <!-- System 1 = fast = curved, repeated, chaotic overlapping arcs -->
    <g fill="none" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.75">
      <path d="M-100 600 Q 400 200 900 600 T 1900 600"/>
      <path d="M-100 700 Q 400 300 900 700 T 1900 700" opacity="0.6"/>
      <path d="M-100 800 Q 400 400 900 800 T 1900 800" opacity="0.45"/>
      <path d="M-100 500 Q 400 100 900 500 T 1900 500" opacity="0.3"/>
    </g>
    <text x="126" y="180" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="18" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em">SYSTEM 1 · FAST</text>
  </g>
  <g clip-path="url(#botHalf)">
    <!-- System 2 = slow = strict grid, axis lines, faint dots -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6">
      <line x1="0" y1="1400" x2="1600" y2="1400"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="1600" x2="1600" y2="1600"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="1800" x2="1600" y2="1800"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="2000" x2="1600" y2="2000"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="2200" x2="1600" y2="2200"/>
      <line x1="400" y1="1230" x2="400" y2="2400"/>
      <line x1="800" y1="1230" x2="800" y2="2400"/>
      <line x1="1200" y1="1230" x2="1200" y2="2400"/>
    </g>
    <g fill="#4c4c4c" opacity="0.7">
      <circle cx="400" cy="1600" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="800" cy="1600" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="1200" cy="1600" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="400" cy="1800" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="800" cy="1800" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="1200" cy="1800" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="400" cy="2000" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="800" cy="2000" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="1200" cy="2000" r="3"/>
    </g>
    <text x="126" y="1320" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="18" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em">SYSTEM 2 · SLOW</text>
  </g>
  <!-- The seam = the red hairline (matches redRule: top — but the seam is the divider between the two halves so we draw it manually here) -->
  <line x1="0" y1="1200" x2="1600" y2="1200" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
</svg>
rationale: Cover is split top/bottom by the literal red hairline — System 1 above (curved, fluid, overlapping, ash) and System 2 below (grid, dots, axis lines, graphite). The 220px mixed-case title is centered ON the seam, so 'Thinking,' sits in the fast half and 'Fast and Slow' sits across the divide. The label 'SYSTEM 1 · FAST' / 'SYSTEM 2 · SLOW' in each half makes the metaphor legible. The pill '1 / 2' lives top-right. No additional accents — the red seam IS the only chromatic element.
